skill.name: artifacts-builder
skill.description: Build complex Claude HTML artifacts with a React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui project workflow.
skill.summary: Scaffold and bundle multi-component Claude HTML artifacts with React, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the alleged weak cryptography findings are false positives from license text and Tailwind color names. The confirmed risk is operational: the skill runs shell scripts, installs npm packages, writes project files, extracts a bundled archive, and removes build output. No evidence found of credential theft, hidden network exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts.

medium_findings.title: Package Manager Commands Install External Dependencies
medium_findings.description: The initialization and bundling scripts run npm and pnpm commands, including a global pnpm install when pnpm is missing. This is expected for a frontend scaffolding skill, but it can modify the host environment and depends on external package registries.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct package manager commands are present and are required for the skill workflow. Confidence is high for operational risk, but no malicious endpoint or exfiltration behavior was found.

medium_findings.title: Bundled Archive Extracted Without Validation
medium_findings.description: The initialization script extracts a local shadcn components archive into the generated project without listing or validating archive paths first. A compromised archive could overwrite project files during setup.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tar extraction behavior is explicit and unvalidated in the script. The risk depends on the integrity of the bundled archive, so confidence is high but not absolute.

medium_findings.title: Generated Project Files Are Rewritten Automatically
medium_findings.description: The scripts rewrite configuration, CSS, and TypeScript project files and remove previous build output. This is normal for scaffolding and bundling, but it can overwrite local changes if run in the wrong directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes and cleanup commands are clearly present. The behavior is legitimate in context, but the impact is real if the user runs the scripts carelessly.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged license text and Tailwind theme words as weak cryptographic algorithms. These lines do not implement cryptography and do not create a crypto risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are license prose, metadata, or CSS color names such as destructive. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API use was found at these locations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Alerts Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Apache license text, shadcn/ui documentation, or a generated schema reference. No evidence found that these URLs receive user data or credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or schema references. The scripts use package managers for network access, but these hardcoded URLs are not exfiltration endpoints.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Alert Is A Type Annotation
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged an import expression in a generated Tailwind configuration comment. This is a TypeScript documentation annotation, not executable dynamic code loading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The import text appears inside a heredoc that writes a config file comment. It is not used to load code at runtime in the shell script.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs users to run bash scripts that create projects, install dependencies, and bundle artifacts. Users should review commands before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The execution path is documented and explicit. This is a real dangerous pattern because shell scripts can change the local environment.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Dependency Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts install frontend dependencies from package registries. This creates supply-chain exposure and may change the local development environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The package installation commands are explicit. No malicious package source was found, but registry dependency is an inherent risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Build Output Deletion
dangerous_patterns.description: The bundle script deletes dist and bundle.html before rebuilding. This is scoped to common build output, but it can remove user files with those names.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The rm command is explicit and limited to two relative paths. It is risky only when run from an unintended project directory.

content.value_statement: Complex artifacts need more structure than a single HTML file. This skill scaffolds a React project and bundles it into one shareable Claude artifact.
actual_capabilities: Creates a React and TypeScript project with Vite.
Configures Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui theme tokens.
Installs Radix UI and related shadcn/ui dependencies.
Adds path aliases for imports from the src directory.
Bundles a project into one self-contained HTML artifact.
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or newer and package manager access.
Downloads npm packages during setup and bundling.
Targets complex artifacts, not simple one-file HTML snippets.
Does not audit the generated application for accessibility or security.

use_cases.title: Interactive Product Prototype
use_cases.description: Create a polished React prototype with reusable UI components and local state.

use_cases.title: Data-Rich Internal Tool
use_cases.description: Build a multi-panel artifact with tables, forms, filters, and charts.

use_cases.title: Reusable Frontend Demo
use_cases.description: Package a Vite application into one HTML file for review in Claude.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Artifact
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new artifact project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use artifacts-builder to create a React artifact named customer-dashboard. Keep the first version simple and easy to edit.

prompt_templates.title: Add shadcn/ui Screens
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for structured interface work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-screen artifact with shadcn/ui components, a sidebar, forms, and responsive tables for a support workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Bundle for Sharing
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare a completed project for Claude artifact sharing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Bundle the current React artifact into a single HTML file and tell me where the output is located.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Complex Prototype
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with state, routing, and component reuse.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a production-style analytics artifact with routed views, saved filters, mock data, charts, and a complete empty state system.

output_examples.input: Create a project for a client reporting dashboard.
output_examples.output: A Vite React project is created with Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui components, and path aliases ready for dashboard development.

output_examples.input: Prepare my artifact for Claude sharing.
output_examples.output: The project is built and converted into one bundled HTML file that can be shared as a Claude artifact.

output_examples.input: Make an interactive workflow tool with forms and tabs.
output_examples.output: The generated project includes a component structure suitable for tabs, form controls, validation, and reusable layout sections.

best_practices: Run the scripts inside a dedicated project directory.
Review generated files before adding important local changes.
Use this skill when the artifact needs state, components, or bundling.
anti_patterns: Do not run the bundler from a directory with unrelated build files.
Do not use this skill for a tiny static HTML artifact.
Do not skip dependency review for sensitive or enterprise environments.

faq.question: What does this skill build?
faq.answer: It builds React-based Claude artifacts using Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui.

faq.question: Does it create a single HTML file?
faq.answer: Yes. The bundle script builds the app and inlines assets into bundle.html.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. The setup and bundle scripts install packages from npm-compatible registries.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is it safe to run in any folder?
faq.answer: No. Run it in a dedicated project folder because it writes files and removes build output.

faq.question: When should I avoid this skill?
faq.answer: Avoid it for simple single-file artifacts or environments where package installation is not allowed.
